subtopics: Finding the pH of the following samples by using pH paper/universal indicator: Dilute Hydrochloric Acid, Dilute NaOH solution, Dilute Ethanoic Acid solution, Lemon juice, Water, Dilute Hydrogen Carbonate solution.
Studying the properties of acids and bases (HCl & NaOH) on the basis of their reaction with Litmus solution (Blue/Red), Zinc metal, Solid sodium carbonate.

Performing and observing reactions: Combination reaction, Decomposition reaction, Displacement reaction, Double displacement reaction. Action of water on quicklime, Action of heat on ferrous sulphate crystals, Iron nails kept in copper sulphate solution, Reaction between sodium sulphate and barium chloride solutions.
Observing the action of Zn, Fe, Cu and Al metals on salt solutions: ZnSO4 (aq), FeSO4 (aq), CuSO4 (aq), Al2(SO4)3 (aq). Arranging Zn, Fe, Cu and Al in decreasing order of reactivity.

topics: Explores the world of matter, its interactions, and properties at the atomic level

chapters.chapter_name: Explores the physical world around them, and understands scientific principles and laws based on observations and analysis
chapters.source_chapter_name: Explores the physical world around them, and understands scientific principles and laws based on observations and analysis
topics: Applies Newton's laws to explain the effect of forces

chapters.chapter_name: Explores the structure and function of the living world at the cellular level
chapters.source_chapter_name: Explores the structure and function of the living world at the cellular level
topics: Explains the role of cellular components
Control and co-ordination in animals and plants
Reflection of light by curved surfaces

subtopics: Electric current, potential difference and electric current. Ohm's law; Resistance, Resistivity, Factors on which the resistance of a conductor depends.
Series combination of resistors, parallel combination of resistors and its applications in daily life.
Heating effect of electric current and its applications in daily life.
Electric power, Interrelation between P, V, I and R.

Magnetic effects of current: Magnetic field, field lines, field due to a current carrying conductor, field due to current carrying coil or solenoid; Force on current carrying conductor, Fleming's Left Hand Rule, Direct current. Alternating current: frequency of AC. Advantage of AC over DC. Domestic electric circuits.
Our environment: Eco-system, Environmental problems, Ozone depletion, waste production and their solutions.
Studying the dependence of potential difference (V) across a resistor on the current (I) passing through it and determine its resistance. Also plotting a graph between V and I.
